old_value: {"id":"project-LICHEN-duvg","title":"[cross-impl] announce message channel field naming inconsistency","description":"Python messages.py:8 updated comment to current_channel (rx_channel) per CCP-9 but Zephyr lichen/lib and Rust lora-link still use rx_channel in packing/signed_data. Different field names for same byte lead to divergent signed_data() behavior across impls. Test vectors ccp9*.json may not cover all. Matches spec/05-routing.md but code diverges.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"agent@lichen-project.dev","created_at":"2026-07-23T04:04:55.269293924Z","created_by":"AI Agent","updated_at":"2026-07-23T04:04:55.269293924Z"}
new_value:
{"notes":"THE RIGHT THING:
Field name: rx_channel (not current_channel)
- Rust uses rx_channel (rust/lichen-core/src/announce.rs:57)
- Python comment says "current_channel (rx_channel)" - confusing, should just use rx_channel
- Test vectors use rx_channel
- CCP-9 spec defines this as the RX channel for rendezvous

FIX:
1. Python: rename any current_channel refs to rx_channel in messages.py
2. Zephyr: verify using rx_channel
3. All: signed_data() must use same field name for identical bytes
4. Validate: ccp9*.json vectors must pass all 3 impls"}
